title: WIP: Lock containers while retrieving them
body:
We were seeing issues where network namespaces were being retrieved while they were in the process of being deleted. Prevent this by locking containers while retrieving them from the database.

Fixes #1168

comments.body: This fixes a potentially confusing corner case in our locking strategy - now we can guarantee we get a good copy of the container state.
Wait, damn - hold off on merging. There's a lock ordering issue around container deletion that can cause a deadlock.
We would need to take the container or pod lock before the database lock to ensure proper ordering. That's problematic, especially for things like AllContainers and AllPods.
Will think about this more on Monday.

Alright, I think I know how to handle this better - we just have to not sync state on the initial pull from the database, and let the subsequent syncContainer() calls handle matters. Easier than trying to develop a locking scheme that allows us to reliably synchronize deletion and retrieval.

Going to close this one now.
